---
title: "Initial Visualizations"
format: html
---

Users can select scene 1 up to scene x.
Summary of scenes/timeline maybe for what's happened in the movie up to scene x?
For each group of scenes, network graph of characters (relative to movie happenings up to scene x).
Can click on node of network graph to get character description, or branch (connection) to get description of relationships.
Network graph will have branches based on romantic, familial, coworker/acquaintance, friend relationships.

```{r}
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)

adjacencies <- read_csv("data/love_actually_adjacencies.csv")

appearances <- read_csv("data/love_actually_appearances.csv")

appearances <- appearances |>
  ## only keep rows with data, after 71 everything is NA b/c there are no more scenes
  slice(1:71) |>
  mutate(scene_number = row_number()) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = 2:15, names_to = "actors", values_to = "appearances") |>
  mutate(appearances = if_else(appearances == "TRUE", 1, 0)) |>
  replace_na(list(appearances = 0)) |>
  mutate(appearances = as.factor(appearances),
         actors = gsub("_", " ", actors),
         actors = str_to_title(actors),
         actors = fct_reorder(actors, appearances == "1", sum))

ggplot(data = appearances, 
       aes(x = scene_number, 
           y = actors, color = appearances)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Appearance:",
                     values = c("0" = "lightcyan2", "1" = "mediumvioletred"),
                     labels = c("0" = "No", "1" = "Yes")) +
  labs(title = "Scene Appearances",
       x = "Scene Number",
       y = "Actor")
  

ggplot(data = appearances |>
         group_by(actors) |>
         filter(appearances == "1") |>
         summarise(num_scenes = n()), 
       aes(x = actors, y = 1, xend = actors, yend = num_scenes)) +
  geom_segment(color = "thistle") +
  geom_point(aes(y = num_scenes), color = "turquoise3") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Total Appearances",
       x = "Actor",
       y = "Number of Scenes")
```
